Professor View 01 / 40
Business English -- 60 Aulas

Maria Claudia Curimbaba

De executiva que traduz mentalmente para o inglês a líder que conduz reuniões internacionais com autoridade e precisão

B1+ / B2 inicial São Paulo, SP Gestora / Sócia de Holding Familiar 60 min / 2x semana / Presencial
Progresso Geral0%
Speed:
Maria Claudia Curimbaba
47 anos
Gestora / Sócia de Holding Familiar
São Paulo, SP
B1+ / B2 inicial
Business English — Reuniões e Negociação
60 aulas de 60 min
Presencial — 2x/semana

Jornada de Transformação

De: Executiva que traduz mentalmente do português para o inglês, perde o timing em reuniões internacionais e depende de colegas para intermediar conversas com parceiros em Houston e Pittsburgh

Para: Líder que conduz reuniões com sócios e parceiros internacionais, negocia contratos, apresenta resultados financeiros e toma decisões estratégicas em inglês com autoridade e precisão

Promessa do Programa

Em 60 aulas, Maria Claudia vai deixar de traduzir mentalmente e passar a pensar, reagir e decidir em inglês. Vai conduzir reuniões com Houston e Pittsburgh, negociar contratos de joint venture e apresentar resultados no board com a mesma autoridade que já tem em português.

Forças

  • Forte experiência em gestão e negociação
  • Conhecimento profundo do vocabulário de negócios em português
  • Motivação alta: precisa do inglês para o dia a dia
  • Exposição regular a parceiros anglófonos
  • Disciplinada e comprometida com o processo

Pontos de Desenvolvimento

  • Tradução mental constante (português para inglês)
  • Vocabulário corporativo limitado em inglês
  • Insegurança ao falar em reuniões formais
  • Dificuldade com voz passiva e estruturas complexas
  • Pronúncia de termos técnicos financeiros
#Tema da AulaFoco LinguísticoAtividade PrincipalHomework

3Running the Meeting — Opening, Agenda, Action ItemsImperatives, let us + verbMeeting opening simulationWrite meeting agenda

20Review and Integration — Block 2All structures reviewedFull negotiation scenarioSelf-assessment recording

27Logistics and Shipping — Industrial VocabularyPassive with modalsLogistics problem-solvingLabel logistics flowchart
28Presentation Skills — Structuring Your MessageDiscourse markers5-minute presentationPrepare presentation outline
29Q and A Mastery — Fielding Difficult QuestionsHedging languageQ and A hot seat simulationWrite anticipated Q and A
30Review and Integration — Block 3All structures reviewedFull presentation and Q and ASelf-assessment recording

32Conflict Resolution — Diplomatic DisagreementI understand howeverConflict mediation RPRecord diplomatic response

34Banking English — Global Financial PartnersInvestment vocabularyBanking meeting simulationWrite investment summary

36Conditional Mastery — If We Shipped by FridayMixed conditionalsConditional negotiationWrite conditional proposals
37Exhibitions and Trade Shows — Networking at EventsColloquial English, small talkGallery opening + trade show RPRecord elevator pitch

40Review and Integration — Block 4All structures reviewedFull simulation: Houston dinner + gallerySelf-assessment recording
41Advanced Passive — News Reports Executive SummariesAll passive formsNews summary practiceWrite executive summary

43Board Meetings — High-Stakes CommunicationFormal registerBoard meeting simulationWrite board presentation notes

Welcome to Your English Journey

Maria Claudia, this is your Pre-class space. Complete these exercises before each lesson to maximize your learning. If you ever get stuck during a conversation, use these emergency phrases:

1 Could you repeat that, please?
2 I am not sure I understand. Could you explain?
3 Let me think about that for a moment.
4 How do you say this in English?
5 Could you speak more slowly, please?
Aula 01 — Pre-class

Who Is Maria Claudia? — Diagnostic + Executive Self-Portrait

Build your executive vocabulary and practice introducing yourself in English
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary
Oversee.to manage and supervise from a high position
"I oversee the financial and sales operations of the holding."
Holding (Company).a parent company that owns other companies
"Our holding company owns three subsidiaries in different sectors."
Subsidiary.a company owned or controlled by a parent company
"The subsidiary in Houston reports directly to us."
Counterpart.a person in the same position at another company
"My counterpart in Pittsburgh handles manufacturing operations."
Stakeholder.a person with an interest in the business
"All stakeholders must approve the annual budget."
Joint Venture.a business arrangement between two companies sharing ownership
"We are planning a joint venture with a Texas-based company."
Procurement.the process of buying goods or services for a business
"The procurement process takes about three months."
Revenue.the total income a company generates
"Our revenue increased by twelve percent last quarter."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its correct definition.

Oversee
Holding (Company)
Subsidiary
Counterpart
Stakeholder
Joint Venture
Procurement
Revenue

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Maria Claudia oversees the financial operations of a family holding company in Sao Paulo. She is responsible for three subsidiaries that operate in different sectors. Every month, she deals with her counterparts in Houston and Pittsburgh to review revenue targets and procurement contracts. The stakeholders expect quarterly reports, and Maria Claudia is currently working on a joint venture with a new partner in Texas.

1. What does Maria Claudia oversee?
A The marketing department
B The financial operations of the holding
C Only the Houston subsidiary
2. How often does she deal with her counterparts?
A Every week
B Every day
C Every month
3. What is Maria Claudia currently working on?
A A new marketing campaign
B Closing the Pittsburgh office
C A joint venture with a Texas partner

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar
Present Simple vs Present Continuous
Present Simple Habitual actions, routines, facts
I oversee the operations. Always, as a routine — habitual action
She deals with partners. Regular, repeated activity
Present Continuous Current projects, temporary actions
I am working on a joint venture. Right now, temporary project
We are planning an expansion. Current, ongoing project

Tip: Use the Present Simple for what you do regularly (routine) and the Present Continuous for what is happening right now or as a temporary project. Key words: always, usually, every day = Present Simple. Currently, right now, at the moment = Present Continuous.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
"The holding company all subsidiaries."
"She with stakeholders on a weekly basis."
"The department reports to Maria Claudia."
" from the Houston subsidiary grew last year."
"Her in Pittsburgh manages the factory."
"They are planning a new this year."
"The needs to increase its revenue."
"All approved the new budget."

Stage 2: Vocabulary Practice — Meeting Introduction

Order

Put these phrases in the correct order to form a professional meeting introduction.

? "I deal with international partners in Houston and Pittsburgh."
? "Good morning. Let me introduce myself."
? "I am currently working on expanding our joint ventures."
? "I am responsible for managing three subsidiaries."
? "Our revenue grew twelve percent last quarter."
? "I am Maria Claudia. I oversee the financial and sales operations of our holding."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
I oversee the operations of our holding company.
I am responsible for three subsidiaries in different sectors.
I deal with counterparts in Houston and Pittsburgh every month.
Our revenue grew twelve percent last quarter.
I am currently working on a joint venture with a Texas partner.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. You meet a new partner at a business dinner. How do you introduce your role?
A "I work with some companies."
B "I oversee the financial and sales operations of our holding company."
C "I am the boss of everything."
D "I do business things in Brazil."
2. Your Houston counterpart asks about your current projects. What do you say?
A "I work on something."
B "I am doing many things now."
C "I am currently working on expanding our joint ventures in the region."
D "I currently work on expand the joint ventures."
3. A stakeholder asks about revenue. Which is the most professional answer?
A "Money is good this year."
B "Our revenue increased by twelve percent last quarter."
C "We make more money than before."
D "The revenue is being good."
4. Someone asks where you work. What is the best response?
A "I am based in Sao Paulo, and I manage operations across three subsidiaries."
B "I am in Sao Paulo."
C "My city is Sao Paulo."
D "I live and work in the Brazil."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection
Imagine you are at a meeting with a new partner from Houston. Introduce yourself and your company in 2 minutes. Include: your name, your role, what you oversee, your subsidiaries, and what you are currently working on.

Survival Card — Lesson 1

1 Let me introduce myself. I am Maria Claudia from Sao Paulo.
2 I oversee the financial and sales operations of our holding.
3 I am responsible for managing three subsidiaries.
4 I deal with international partners in Houston and Pittsburgh.
5 I am currently working on expanding our joint ventures.
Aula 02 — Pre-class

The Holding Structure — Describing Your Business Empire

Learn to describe your company structure with precision
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary
Headquarters.the main office where a company is managed from
"Our headquarters is located in downtown Sao Paulo."
Branch.a local office or division of a larger company
"We have two branches in the United States."
Board (of Directors).the group of people who govern a company
"The board of directors approved the expansion plan."
Shareholder.a person who owns part of a company
"Each shareholder receives a quarterly dividend report."
Merge.to combine two companies into one
"The two companies decided to merge last year."
Acquire.to buy or take over another company
"We plan to acquire a logistics company in Texas."
Division.a major section or department of a company
"The technology division is our fastest-growing unit."
Assets.valuable properties or resources owned by a company
"The company's assets include real estate and patents."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its correct English definition.

Headquarters
Branch
Board
Shareholder
Merge
Acquire
Division
Assets

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar
Maria Claudia is presenting her holding company to a potential partner from Houston. She explains that the company's headquarters is located in Sao Paulo. The board of directors meets every quarter to review the company's assets and approve new investments. There are three main divisions under the holding. The technology division is the largest, and there is a new logistics branch in Texas. The company's shareholders voted to acquire a small firm in Pittsburgh last month. There are also plans to merge two of the smaller subsidiaries into one stronger division.
1. Where is the company's headquarters located?
A Houston
B Sao Paulo
C Pittsburgh
D Texas
2. How many main divisions are there under the holding?
A Two
B Four
C Three
D Five
3. What did the shareholders vote to do last month?
A Merge the headquarters
B Close a branch in Texas
C Acquire a small firm in Pittsburgh
D Sell the company's assets

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar
Possessives: 's and s'
Rule Example
Singular noun + 's The company's headquarters = the headquarters of the company
Plural noun ending in s + ' The shareholders' meeting = the meeting of the shareholders
Irregular plural + 's The board's decision = the decision of the board
There is / There are
Structure Usage Example
There is Singular nouns There is a new branch in Texas.
There are Plural nouns There are three divisions under the holding.

Key Expressions

Vocabulary
Our headquarters is located in...
"Our headquarters is located in Sao Paulo, and we manage operations across three countries."
The board is responsible for...
"The board is responsible for approving major investments and strategic decisions."
We have branches in...
"We have branches in Houston, Pittsburgh, and soon in Miami."

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Use the hints if you need help.

"The holding's is in Sao Paulo."
"There three divisions under the main company."
"The decision was to acquire a new firm."
"There a new branch in Texas."
"The meeting is scheduled for June."
"The company's include patents and real estate."
"There two branches in the United States."
"The revenue grew by fifteen percent."

Stage 2: Vocabulary Practice

Order

Put these sentences in the correct order to form a company description presentation.

? "The board of directors meets quarterly to review our performance."
? "Good morning. Let me describe our company structure."
? "We are currently looking to acquire a new logistics firm."
? "There are three main divisions: technology, logistics, and finance."
? "Our headquarters is located in Sao Paulo, Brazil."
? "We have branches in Houston and Pittsburgh."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself. Aim for 80% accuracy or higher.

Our headquarters is located in downtown Sao Paulo, Brazil.
The board of directors is responsible for approving all major investments.
There are three divisions under our holding company.
We have branches in Houston, Pittsburgh, and Texas.
The shareholders approved the plan to acquire a new firm.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. You are at a conference and an investor asks about your company location. What do you say?
A "We have assets in many places."
B "Our headquarters is located in Sao Paulo."
C "The board decided to move last year."
D "We merged with a Sao Paulo company."
2. A partner asks who makes the big decisions at your company. What do you answer?
A "The branch in Houston handles everything."
B "Our assets decide the strategy."
C "The board of directors is responsible for all major decisions."
D "We acquire companies to make decisions."
3. You want to explain that your company has offices in several cities. What is the best way to say it?
A "There is branches in many cities."
B "We merged branches in Houston."
C "The division has one office."
D "We have branches in Houston, Pittsburgh, and Miami."
4. Your company recently bought another company. How do you describe this to a colleague?
A "We merged our headquarters with Texas."
B "The shareholders are new assets."
C "We acquired a logistics company in Texas last quarter."
D "The board divided the branch."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection
Imagine you are at a conference. A potential investor asks: "Can you describe your company structure?" Record yourself describing your holding in 2 minutes. Try to use: headquarters, branch, board, shareholders, divisions, and assets.

Survival Card — Lesson 2

1 Our headquarters is located in Sao Paulo.
2 The holding has three subsidiaries.
3 The board meets quarterly to review our assets.
4 We are looking to acquire a new company.
5 There are branches in Houston and Pittsburgh.
Aula 03 — Pre-class

Running the Meeting

Master meeting vocabulary: agenda, minutes, action items, deadlines. Learn to open, facilitate, and close meetings with confidence.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary

Vocabulary
Agenda • a list of topics to discuss in a meeting
"We need to review the agenda before the meeting starts."
Minutes • the official written record of a meeting
"Please send the minutes to all participants by end of day."
Action item • a specific task assigned to someone during a meeting
"Each action item must have a clear owner and deadline."
Deadline • the final date by which something must be completed
"The deadline for the procurement report is next Friday."
Adjourn • to formally end a meeting
"If there are no more questions, let's adjourn."
Facilitate • to guide or manage a discussion or meeting
"Maria Claudia will facilitate the quarterly review today."
Follow up • to check on progress or take further action after a meeting
"I will follow up on the pending items next Monday."
Wrap up • to bring a discussion or meeting to a close
"Let's wrap up this discussion and move to the next topic."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Agenda
Minutes
Action item
Deadline
Adjourn
Facilitate
Follow up
Wrap up

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar
Maria Claudia is facilitating a quarterly review meeting with her team and counterparts from Houston. She opens the meeting by reviewing the agenda. "Let's begin with the revenue update. Please look at the numbers on your screen. Let's move on to the procurement report. Take note of the new deadlines. Each action item must have an owner. Please send the minutes by end of day. Let's adjourn and follow up next week."
1. What is Maria Claudia doing in this meeting?
A Presenting her holding company
B Facilitating a quarterly review
C Negotiating a contract
D Interviewing a new employee
2. What does Maria Claudia ask the team to do with the minutes?
A Read them before the meeting
B Delete them after the meeting
C Send them by end of day
D Print them for the next meeting
3. What must each action item have?
A A budget
B A presentation
C An owner
D A separate meeting

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar
Imperatives: Giving Instructions
Structure Usage Example
Base verb (direct) Clear command Take note of the action items.
Please + verb (polite) Professional request Please send the minutes by Friday.
Do not + verb (negative) Prohibition Do not forget the deadline.
Let's + Verb: Group Suggestions
Structure Usage Example
Let's + verb Positive suggestion Let's review the agenda.
Let's not + verb Negative suggestion Let's not skip any items.

Key Expressions

Vocabulary
Let's move on to...
"Let's move on to the procurement report and review the new deadlines."
Let's wrap up...
"Let's wrap up the budget discussion and assign action items to each team."
Please make sure to...
"Please make sure to send the updated numbers to Houston before Thursday."

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Use the hints if you need help.

"Let's begin with the first on the agenda."
"Please send the by end of day."
"Let's this discussion and move to the next topic."
" note of the action items."
"The for the procurement report is next Friday."
"Maria Claudia will the meeting today."
"Let's on the pending items next Monday."
"If there are no more questions, let's ."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Put these meeting steps in the correct order.

? "Let's begin with the revenue update from Q3."
? "Let's assign action items before we finish."
? "Good morning, everyone. Let's get started."
? "Please send the minutes by end of day. Meeting adjourned."
? "First, let's review the agenda for today."
? "Please take note of the new procurement deadlines."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen, then record yourself saying each phrase. Compare your pronunciation.

Let's begin with the first item on the agenda.
Please send the minutes to all participants by end of day.
Let's move on to the next topic on the agenda.
Take note of the action items and their deadlines.
Let's adjourn. I will follow up on Friday.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. You are opening a meeting. What do you say?
A "I am responsible for the financial operations."
B "Let's begin with the agenda for today."
C "There are three divisions under the holding."
D "Our headquarters is located in Sao Paulo."
2. You want the team to discuss the next topic. What do you say?
A "The deadline is next Friday."
B "Please adjourn the meeting."
C "Let's move on to the procurement report."
D "I deal with international partners."
3. You need someone to send the meeting notes. What do you say?
A "Let's acquire a new firm."
B "Take note of the shareholders."
C "There is a new branch in Texas."
D "Please send the minutes by Friday."
4. You want to end the meeting. What do you say?
A "Let's follow up next week."
B "If there are no more questions, let's adjourn."
C "Our revenue increased by twelve percent."
D "I am currently working on a joint venture."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection
Imagine you are facilitating a quarterly review meeting with your Houston counterpart. How would you open the meeting, introduce the agenda, and assign action items? Record your response.

Survival Card — Lesson 3

1 Let's begin with the first item on the agenda.
2 Could we move on to the next topic?
3 Please send the minutes by end of day.
4 Let's assign action items before we adjourn.
5 I will follow up on this by Friday.
Aula 04 — Pre-class

Money Talks

Master financial vocabulary: revenue, profit, budget, forecast. Learn to compare numbers and present financial results.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary

Vocabulary
Revenue • the total income a company earns from sales
"Our revenue increased by twelve percent last quarter."
Profit • the money remaining after all expenses are paid
"The company reported a net profit of two million dollars."
Budget • a financial plan that outlines expected income and spending
"We need to approve the budget for next quarter."
Forecast • a prediction of future financial performance
"The forecast shows steady growth through Q4."
Expenses • the costs of running a business
"Our operating expenses are lower than last year."
Growth • an increase in value, revenue, or market size
"We achieved ten percent growth compared to Q2."
Margin • the percentage difference between revenue and costs
"Our profit margin improved from eight to twelve percent."
Quarter • a three-month period in the business year (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4)
"Q3 results exceeded our expectations."

Expressions

Vocabulary
Compared to last quarter...
"Compared to last quarter, our revenue is up twelve percent."
The numbers show that...
"The numbers show that our profit margin has improved significantly."
We are on track to...
"We are on track to exceed our annual revenue target."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Revenue
Profit
Budget
Forecast
Expenses
Growth
Margin
Quarter

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar
Maria Claudia is presenting the Q3 financial results to the board. Revenue is higher than Q2 by twelve percent. The profit margin improved from eight percent to twelve percent. Operating expenses are lower than last year because the procurement team negotiated better contracts. The Houston subsidiary showed the strongest growth, with revenue up fifteen percent compared to Q2. The forecast for Q4 is positive, and the budget has been approved. Overall, Q3 results are better than expected.
1. How does Q3 revenue compare to Q2?
A It is lower than Q2
B It is higher than Q2 by twelve percent
C It is the same as Q2
D It decreased compared to Q2
2. Why are operating expenses lower than last year?
A The company reduced its workforce
B They moved to a smaller office
C The procurement team negotiated better contracts
D The Houston subsidiary closed
3. Which subsidiary showed the strongest growth?
A The Pittsburgh subsidiary
B The Houston subsidiary
C The Texas subsidiary
D The Sao Paulo headquarters

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar
Comparatives: Comparing Numbers and Results
Structure Usage Example
higher than Compare larger values Revenue is higher than last quarter.
lower than Compare smaller values Expenses are lower than last year.
better than Compare quality/results Q3 results are better than expected.
worse than Compare negative results Q1 performance was worse than Q4.
more/less + noun + than Compare quantities We have more revenue than last year.
as + adj + as Compare equal values Q3 was as strong as we expected.
Numbers in Financial English
Term Example
percent twelve percent growth
million two million dollars
billion one billion in revenue
point (decimal) eight point five percent

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
"Our revenue is than last quarter."
"Operating decreased by five percent."
"The profit improved from eight to twelve percent."
"The for Q4 shows steady growth."
"We need to approve the for next year."
"Q3 exceeded two million dollars."
"The Houston subsidiary achieved fifteen percent ."
"Q3 results are than expected."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order
? "Our operating expenses were lower than expected."
? "First, our total revenue for Q3 was twelve million dollars."
? "The forecast for Q4 shows continued growth."
? "Good morning. Let's review the Q3 financial results."
? "The profit margin improved from eight to twelve percent."
? "Compared to Q2, that is an increase of twelve percent."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
Our revenue increased by twelve percent compared to last quarter.
The profit margin is higher than Q2, at twelve percent.
Operating expenses are lower than last year.
We are on track to exceed our annual targets.
The forecast for Q4 shows steady growth.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. Your revenue went from 10M to 12M. How do you describe this?
A "Our revenue decreased this quarter."
B "Our revenue is higher than last quarter."
C "Our revenue stayed the same."
2. Expenses went from 5M to 4M. What happened?
A "Our expenses are higher than before."
B "Our expenses are lower than last year."
C "Our expenses remained unchanged."
3. You want to show Q4 predictions. What do you present?
A "The budget for Q4."
B "The forecast for Q4."
C "The expenses for Q4."
4. The board asks about profitability. What do you report?
A "Our revenue increased by twelve percent."
B "Our profit margin improved to twelve percent."
C "Our budget was approved for next quarter."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection
Imagine you are presenting the Q3 financial results to the board. Describe the revenue, expenses, profit margin, and forecast. Use comparatives to show how Q3 compares to Q2.

Survival Card — Lesson 4

1 Our revenue increased by twelve percent this quarter.
2 Compared to last year, our profit margin is higher.
3 The budget for next quarter is three million dollars.
4 We are on track to meet our annual targets.
5 The forecast shows steady growth for Q4.
Aula 05 — Pre-class

Colors and Opinions

Describing Art, Expressing Preferences. Learn adjective order (OSASCOMP) and how to express opinions using "I find it + adjective".
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary

Vocabulary
Striking • very attractive or noticeable
"The painting has a striking use of color."
Abstract • art that does not represent real objects
"She prefers abstract art over realistic paintings."
Exhibit • a public display of art or objects
"The museum has a new exhibit on modern art."
Contemporary • belonging to the present time
"Contemporary art often challenges traditional ideas."
Vibrant • bright and strong, full of energy
"The artist uses vibrant colors like red and orange."
Subtle • not obvious, delicate and understated
"The subtle tones in the background create depth."
Composition • the arrangement of elements in a work of art
"The composition draws your eye to the center."
Palette • the range of colors used by an artist
"The artist chose a warm palette of earth tones."
Expressions
I find it...
"I find it fascinating how the artist uses light and shadow."
What strikes me is...
"What strikes me is the contrast between the warm and cool colors."
In my opinion...
"In my opinion, this is one of the most powerful pieces in the exhibit."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Striking
Abstract
Exhibit
Contemporary
Vibrant
Subtle
Composition
Palette

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar
Maria Claudia is visiting a contemporary art exhibit in Houston with her counterpart David Chen. She stops in front of a large abstract painting with vibrant colors. "I find it fascinating," she says. "What strikes me is the bold composition. The artist uses a warm palette of reds and oranges. In my opinion, this is the most striking piece in the exhibit." David points to a smaller, more subtle painting nearby. "I find it elegant," he says. "The composition is simpler, but the colors are more refined. It is a beautiful old European oil painting."
1. What does Maria Claudia think about the large abstract painting?
A She finds it boring
B She finds it fascinating
C She finds it too simple
D She does not like the colors
2. What palette does the artist of the large painting use?
A A cool palette of blues and greens
B A warm palette of reds and oranges
C A neutral palette of grays and whites
D A dark palette of blacks and browns
3. How does David describe the smaller painting?
A He finds it striking and bold
B He finds it elegant with refined colors
C He finds it confusing and abstract
D He does not like it at all

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar
Adjective Order (OSASCOMP)
Order Category Example
1Opinionbeautiful, striking, elegant
2Sizelarge, small, tiny
3Ageold, new, contemporary
4Shaperound, rectangular, abstract
5Colorred, vibrant, dark
6OriginEuropean, Japanese, Brazilian
7Materialoil, watercolor, acrylic
8Purposedecorative, commemorative
"I find it + adjective"
Structure Example
I find it + adjectiveI find it beautiful.
I find it + adjective + how/thatI find it interesting how the artist uses light.
I find it + adjective (opinion)I find it thought-provoking.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
"I find it how the artist uses light."
"The painting has a use of color and texture."
"She prefers art over realistic paintings."
"The museum has a new on modern art."
"The artist uses colors like red and orange."
"The tones in the background create depth."
"The draws your eye to the center of the painting."
"The artist chose a warm of earth tones."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order
? "What strikes me is the vibrant palette the artist chose."
? "Let me know which painting you find most interesting."
? "Welcome to the exhibit. Let me show you around."
? "In my opinion, this is the most striking piece in the collection."
? "This is a contemporary abstract painting from 2023."
? "I find it fascinating how the composition guides your eye."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
I find it fascinating how the artist uses light and shadow.
What strikes me is the contrast between warm and cool colors.
The exhibit features contemporary abstract paintings.
In my opinion, the composition is very powerful.
The artist chose a vibrant palette of reds and oranges.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. You see a painting with bold, bright colors. How do you describe it?
A "The painting has subtle colors."
B "The painting has vibrant colors."
C "The painting has no colors."
2. Someone asks your opinion about art. How do you respond?
A "I have no opinion about it."
B "I find it thought-provoking."
C "The revenue increased last quarter."
3. You want to describe the arrangement of elements. What word do you use?
A "The palette is well-balanced."
B "The composition is well-balanced."
C "The exhibit is well-balanced."
4. You visit a museum display. What is it called?
A "An exhibit."
B "A palette."
C "A composition."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection
Imagine you are visiting an art exhibit with your Houston counterpart. Describe your favorite painting: the colors, the composition, and why you find it interesting.

Survival Card — Lesson 5

1 I find it fascinating.
2 What strikes me is the use of color.
3 In my opinion, this is a beautiful piece.
4 The artist uses a vibrant palette.
5 The composition is very striking.
Aula 06 — Pre-class

The Passive Voice

Reports Are Written, Paintings Are Exhibited. Learn the present passive (is/are + past participle) to describe processes and procedures.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary

Vocabulary
Submit • to present a document or report for review
"Reports are submitted to the board every quarter."
Approve • to officially accept or agree to something
"The budget is approved by the shareholders."
Review • to examine or assess something carefully
"Financial statements are reviewed by the auditors."
Assign • to give someone a specific task or responsibility
"Action items are assigned at the end of each meeting."
Display • to show or exhibit something publicly
"Contemporary paintings are displayed in the main gallery."
Commission • to formally request someone to create a work of art
"New artworks are commissioned by the museum every year."
Distribute • to share or deliver something to multiple people
"Meeting minutes are distributed to all participants."
Evaluate • to assess the quality or value of something
"Employee performance is evaluated twice a year."
Expressions
It is done by...
"The report is reviewed by the finance team before submission."
It is considered...
"This painting is considered one of the most important works of the century."
It has been...
"The budget has been approved by the board."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Submit
Approve
Review
Assign
Display
Commission
Distribute
Evaluate

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar
Maria Claudia is explaining how her company operates to a new partner. Reports are submitted to the board every quarter. The annual budget is approved by the shareholders in January. Financial statements are reviewed by external auditors. Action items are assigned at the end of each meeting, and meeting minutes are distributed to all participants within 24 hours. On the art side, Maria Claudia explains that contemporary paintings are displayed in the company's lobby. New artworks are commissioned every two years. Each piece is evaluated by a committee before it is purchased.
1. How often are reports submitted to the board?
A Every month
B Every quarter
C Every year
D Every week
2. Who approves the annual budget?
A The auditors
B The shareholders
C The committee
D The museum
3. How often are new artworks commissioned?
A Every quarter
B Every year
C Every two years
D Every five years

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar
Present Passive (is/are + past participle)
Active Voice Passive Voice
The board approves the budget.The budget is approved by the board.
The museum displays the paintings.The paintings are displayed in the museum.
The auditors review the statements.The statements are reviewed by the auditors.
They distribute the minutes.The minutes are distributed to all participants.
Structure
Component Example
Subject + is/are + past participleThe budget is approved.
+ by agent (optional)The budget is approved by the board.
Present perfect passiveThe budget has been approved by the board.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
"Reports are to the board every quarter."
"The budget is by the shareholders."
"Financial statements are by the auditors."
"Action items are at the end of each meeting."
"Contemporary paintings are in the main gallery."
"New artworks are by the museum every year."
"Meeting minutes are to all participants."
"Employee performance is twice a year."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order
? "The final version is submitted to the board."
? "The report is written by the finance team."
? "Copies are distributed to all stakeholders."
? "Corrections are made based on the feedback."
? "The report is approved by the shareholders."
? "It is reviewed by the department manager."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
Reports are submitted to the board every quarter.
The budget is approved by the shareholders in January.
Contemporary paintings are displayed in our lobby.
Meeting minutes are distributed within 24 hours.
Each artwork is evaluated before it is purchased.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. Who approves the budget?
A "The budget is approved by the auditors."
B "The budget is approved by the shareholders."
C "The budget is approved by the committee."
2. How are meeting notes shared?
A "Minutes are submitted to the board."
B "Minutes are distributed to all participants."
C "Minutes are evaluated by the manager."
3. Where are the paintings shown?
A "They are displayed in the main gallery."
B "They are submitted to the museum."
C "They are assigned to the committee."
4. How often is performance checked?
A "It is evaluated every quarter."
B "It is evaluated twice a year."
C "It is evaluated every month."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection
Describe how a financial report moves through your company. Use the passive voice: it is written, reviewed, submitted, approved, and distributed.

Survival Card — Lesson 6

1 The report is submitted every quarter.
2 The budget has been approved by the board.
3 Paintings are displayed in the main gallery.
4 Minutes are distributed after every meeting.
5 New artworks are commissioned every two years.
Aula 07 — Pre-class

Negotiation 101

Prices, Terms, Conditions. Learn the first conditional (If + present simple, will + base verb) to propose terms, make counteroffers, and reach agreements.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary

Vocabulary
Negotiate • to discuss terms in order to reach an agreement
"We need to negotiate the price before signing the contract."
Proposal • a formal plan or suggestion presented for consideration
"The vendor submitted a proposal for the new logistics contract."
Counteroffer • a response to an offer with different terms
"We will make a counteroffer if the price is too high."
Discount • a reduction in the usual price
"If you order in bulk, we will offer a ten percent discount."
Terms • the conditions of an agreement or contract
"The payment terms are net 30 days."
Clause • a specific section or condition in a contract
"There is a penalty clause for late delivery."
Leverage • an advantage used to influence a negotiation
"Our volume gives us leverage to negotiate better prices."
Concession • something given up in a negotiation to reach agreement
"We made a concession on the delivery timeline."
Expressions
If you agree to..., we will...
"If you agree to a two-year contract, we will offer a fifteen percent discount."
We are willing to...
"We are willing to extend the payment terms to 60 days."
On the condition that...
"We will accept the price on the condition that delivery is guaranteed by March."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice
Negotiate
Proposal
Counteroffer
Discount
Terms
Clause
Leverage
Concession

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar
Maria Claudia is negotiating a new logistics contract with a vendor in Texas. She reviews the proposal carefully. If the price is too high, she will make a counteroffer. The vendor offers a five percent discount, but Maria Claudia wants ten percent. "If you agree to a two-year contract, we will increase the discount to ten percent," the vendor says. Maria Claudia considers the terms. There is a penalty clause for late delivery, which gives her leverage. "We are willing to sign for two years on the condition that delivery is guaranteed within five business days," she replies. If both sides make concessions, they will reach an agreement.
1. What will Maria Claudia do if the price is too high?
A She will accept the price.
B She will make a counteroffer.
C She will cancel the contract.
D She will ask for a new vendor.
2. What does the vendor offer if Maria Claudia agrees to a two-year contract?
A Free delivery
B A ten percent discount
C Extended payment terms
D A five percent discount
3. What gives Maria Claudia leverage in the negotiation?
A The two-year contract
B The discount percentage
C The penalty clause for late delivery
D The vendor's proposal

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar
First Conditional (If + present simple, will + base verb)
If clause (present simple) Main clause (will + base verb)
If we increase the order,the vendor will offer a discount.
If they accept our terms,we will sign the contract.
If we do not negotiate,we will pay full price.
If the price is right,we will sign the contract.
Structure
Component Example
If + subject + present simpleIf you agree to a two-year contract,
subject + will + base verbwe will offer a fifteen percent discount.
Negative: If + subject + do/does notIf we do not negotiate, we will pay full price.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice
"We need to the price before signing."
"The vendor submitted a for the new contract."
"We will make a if the price is too high."
"If you order in bulk, we will offer a ten percent ."
"The payment are net 30 days."
"There is a penalty for late delivery."
"Our volume gives us to negotiate better prices."
"We made a on the delivery timeline."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order
? "If you offer a ten percent discount, we will sign today."
? "Let me prepare the final contract with the agreed terms."
? "Thank you for your proposal. Let me review the terms."
? "On the condition that delivery is guaranteed, we have a deal."
? "The price seems high. We would like to negotiate."
? "We are willing to extend the contract to two years."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking
If the price is too high, we will make a counteroffer.
We are willing to extend the payment terms to 60 days.
If you agree to a two-year contract, we will offer a discount.
There is a penalty clause for late delivery in the contract.
On the condition that delivery is guaranteed, we will sign.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. The price is too high. What do you do?
A "We will accept the price immediately."
B "We will make a counteroffer."
C "We will cancel the meeting."
2. You want the vendor to lower the price. What do you say?
A "We do not want to negotiate."
B "If you offer a discount, we will increase the order."
C "We will pay full price."
3. You want to add a condition. How do you express it?
A "On the condition that delivery is guaranteed."
B "We do not have conditions."
C "The clause is not important."
4. The vendor asks for a longer contract. What do you offer?
A "We will not sign any contract."
B "We are willing to sign for two years."
C "We need more leverage."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection
Imagine you are negotiating a logistics contract with a Texas vendor. The price is too high. Use first conditionals to propose terms, make a counteroffer, and reach an agreement.

Survival Card — Lesson 7

1 If the price is right, we will sign the contract.
2 We are willing to negotiate the terms.
3 On the condition that delivery is on time, we agree.
4 We would like to make a counteroffer.
5 If both sides make concessions, we will reach a deal.
Aula 8 -- Pre-class

Strategic Proposals -- What If?

Weigh a hypothetical expansion like an executive: build proposals with the second conditional (If we acquired the plant, we would double capacity) and give diplomatic advice (If I were you...). New vocabulary: scenario, contingency, downside, upside, trade-off, outweigh, feasible, mitigate, allocate, prioritize, projection, hypothetical.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Scenario -- a possible situation or sequence of events
"Let us consider a different scenario for the expansion." (Cenário)
Contingency -- a plan for a possible future problem
"We need a contingency in case the deal falls through." (Contingência)
Downside -- the negative side or disadvantage of something
"The downside of the move is the higher cost." (Desvantagem)
Upside -- the positive side or potential advantage
"The upside is a much larger market share." (Vantagem)
Trade-off -- a balance between two things you cannot both have fully
"There is always a trade-off between speed and cost." (Compensação)
Outweigh -- to be greater or more important than something else
"The benefits outweigh the risks." (Superar)
Feasible -- realistically possible to do
"Is the timeline feasible for our team?" (Viável)
Mitigate -- to make something bad less serious
"We can mitigate the risk with a phased rollout." (Mitigar)
Allocate -- to give resources for a specific purpose
"We would allocate more capital to the US plant." (Alocar)
Prioritize -- to put things in order of importance
"We must prioritize the most profitable projects." (Priorizar)
Projection -- an estimate of a future amount or result
"The five-year projection looks promising." (Projeção)
Hypothetical -- based on a possible situation, not a real one
"This is a hypothetical scenario, not a decision." (Hipotético)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Scenario
Contingency
Downside
Upside
Trade-off
Outweigh
Feasible
Mitigate
Allocate
Prioritize
Projection
Hypothetical

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Imagine a hypothetical scenario. If we acquired the Pittsburgh plant, we would double our capacity on the East Coast. The upside would be faster delivery; the downside would be a heavy capital allocation. There is always a trade-off. My projection shows that the benefits would outweigh the costs within three years, but only if demand grew. If I were you, I would move in phases to mitigate the risk. Is that feasible? If the board approved a phased plan, we could prioritize the key contracts first and keep a contingency in reserve.

1. Is the situation in the text real or hypothetical?
A Hypothetical -- imaginary and not yet decided.
B Real -- the plant has already been acquired.
C A past event that already happened.
2. When would the benefits outweigh the costs?
A Immediately, with no conditions.
B Within three years, but only if demand grew.
C Never -- the costs are always higher.
3. What does the writer advise to reduce the risk?
A Move in phases to mitigate the risk and keep a contingency.
B Allocate all the capital at once.
C Cancel the proposal entirely.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The Second Conditional

GRAMMAR

Use o segundo condicional para propostas e cenários HIPOTÉTICOS ou improváveis: if + passado simples, would/could/might + verbo base. / Use the second conditional for HYPOTHETICAL or unlikely proposals and scenarios: if + past simple, would/could/might + base verb.

FunctionPatternExample
Condition (if-clause)if + past simple"If we acquired the plant..."
Resultwould / could / might + base verb"...we would double our capacity"
Were for all subjectsif + I/he/she/it + were"If I were you, I would wait"
QuestionWhat + would + subject + base verb"What would you do if the deal fell through?"

Key point / Ponto-chave: o if-clause usa o passado simples e o resultado usa would/could/might + verbo base. NUNCA use "would" no if-clause (errado: "if I would acquire"; certo: "if I acquired"). Use were para todos os sujeitos (If I were you). O segundo condicional é hipotético/improvável, não real -- diferente do primeiro condicional (if + presente, will), que descreve condições reais.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word or form. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"If we the Pittsburgh plant, we would double our capacity."
"If I were you, I wait for the forecast."
"The benefits clearly the costs."
"We can the risk with a phased move."
"The five-year looks promising."
"Is the timeline for our team?"

Stage 2: Weigh a Proposal in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of weighing a strategic proposal in the correct order.

?You weigh the trade-off and check whether the benefits outweigh the costs.
?You define the scenario and the hypothetical question you want to answer.
?You prioritize the option with the strongest projection and present it.
?You list the upside and the downside of each option.
?You decide how to allocate resources and mitigate the main risks.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

If we acquired the Pittsburgh plant, we would double our capacity.
Se adquiríssemos a fábrica de Pittsburgh, dobraríamos nossa capacidade.
If I were you, I would prioritize the Sao Paulo operation.
Se eu fosse você, priorizaria a operação de São Paulo.
The benefits would outweigh the costs within three years.
Os benefícios superariam os custos em três anos.
What would you do if the deal fell through?
O que você faria se o negócio fracassasse?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want to propose a hypothetical expansion. Which is best?
A "If we acquired the plant, we would double our capacity."
B "If we would acquire the plant, we would double our capacity."
C "If we acquire the plant, we will double our capacity."
You want to give diplomatic advice to a colleague. Which is best?
A "If I were you, I would wait for the forecast."
B "If I was you, I will wait for the forecast."
C "If I am you, I would wait for the forecast."
You want to ask about a worst-case outcome. Which is best?
A "What you would do if the deal fell through?"
B "What would you do if the deal fell through?"
C "What will you do if the deal will fall through?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Propose one hypothetical move for your business. Describe the scenario, the upside and the downside, and the main trade-off, using the second conditional (If we did X, we would...). Then say what would make it feasible and how you would mitigate the risk.

Survival Card -- Lesson 8

1If we acquired the Pittsburgh plant, we would double our capacity.Se adquiríssemos a fábrica de Pittsburgh, dobraríamos nossa capacidade.
2If I were you, I would prioritize the Sao Paulo operation.Se eu fosse você, priorizaria a operação de São Paulo.
3The benefits would outweigh the costs within three years.Os benefícios superariam os custos em três anos.
4We could move in phases to mitigate the risk.Poderíamos avançar em fases para mitigar o risco.
5What would you do if the deal fell through?O que você faria se o negócio fracassasse?
Aula 9 -- Pre-class

Gallery Night -- Socializing About Art & Business

Work a gallery opening like a pro: network and mingle, break the ice, and give your opinion in many ways beyond "I think" -- strong (I'd argue that...), neutral (From my perspective...), or hedged (I'm inclined to think...). New vocabulary: networking, mingle, small talk, break the ice, strike up, curator, collector, vernissage, compelling, thought-provoking, candid, impression.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Networking -- building professional relationships at social events
"A gallery opening is perfect for networking." (Fazer contatos profissionais)
Mingle -- to move around a room and talk with many people
"She loves to mingle and meet new collectors." (Circular/socializar)
Small talk -- light, informal conversation about easy topics
"We made small talk about the wine before the deal came up." (Conversa informal)
Break the ice -- to start a conversation and reduce awkwardness
"A compliment about the art is a good way to break the ice." (Quebrar o gelo)
Strike up -- to begin a conversation with someone
"I struck up a conversation with the collector from Chicago." (Puxar conversa)
Curator -- the person who selects and organizes an exhibition
"The curator chose every piece in the show." (Curador/a)
Collector -- a person who buys and keeps works of art
"The collector already owns three of the artist's works." (Colecionador/a)
Vernissage -- a private preview before an exhibition opens to the public
"We were invited to the vernissage on Thursday night." (Abertura/pré-estreia)
Compelling -- so interesting or powerful that it holds your attention
"The new collection is the most compelling work he has shown." (Cativante)
Thought-provoking -- making you think deeply about an idea
"The big installation is genuinely thought-provoking." (Instigante)
Candid -- honest and direct, even when it is not flattering
"Can I be candid? The early works are stronger." (Franco/sincero)
Impression -- the feeling or opinion something gives you
"What was your first impression of the show?" (Impressão)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Networking
Mingle
Small talk
Break the ice
Strike up
Curator
Collector
Vernissage
Compelling
Thought-provoking
Candid
Impression

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

At the vernissage, David finds me by a painting. "What's your take on the show?" he asks. "From my perspective, it's his strongest yet," I say. "The large installation is genuinely thought-provoking." David is candid: "I'd argue that the early pieces are more compelling, but the curator clearly believes in this direction." "I see your point," I reply, "but I'm inclined to think the market will reward it." Then he spots a collector across the room. "Let's mingle -- this is the networking I had in mind." So I break the ice with some small talk about the art, and the business can wait.

1. How does Maria introduce her opinion of the show?
A With "From my perspective..." -- a neutral, professional phrase.
B With "According to me..."
C She refuses to give any opinion.
2. How does David signal that his view is strong and confident?
A With "I suppose..."
B With "I'd argue that..."
C With "It seems to me..."
3. How does Maria disagree without sounding rude?
A She says "I see your point, but I'm inclined to think..." -- a hedged disagreement.
B She says "You are completely wrong."
C She says nothing and walks away.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The Opinion Toolkit

GRAMMAR

No nível B2, varie suas frases de opinião em vez de repetir "I think". Escolha a FORÇA certa: forte, neutra ou hesitante -- e convide a opinião do outro. / At B2, vary your opinion phrases instead of repeating "I think". Choose the right STRENGTH: strong, neutral, or hedged -- and invite the other person's view.

StrengthPhrasesExample
Strong / confidentI'd argue that... / I have to say..."I'd argue that it is his best work."
Neutral / professionalFrom my perspective... / In my view..."From my perspective, it is striking."
Tentative / hedgedI'm inclined to think... / It seems to me... / I suppose..."I'm inclined to think it will sell."
Invite the other viewWhat's your take on...? / How do you find...?"What's your take on the show?"

Key point / Ponto-chave: escolha a força da frase conforme sua confiança: forte quando tem certeza, hesitante (hedged) para soar diplomático. NUNCA diga "according to me" (use "from my perspective / in my opinion") nem "I am agree" (use "I agree"). E mantenha a conversa viva convidando a opinião do outro com "What's your take on...?". / Choose the strength by your confidence: strong when certain, hedged to sound diplomatic. Never say "according to me" or "I am agree".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word or phrase. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

" my perspective, it's his strongest work yet."
"I'd that the early pieces are more compelling."
"I'm to think the market will reward it."
"What's your on the large installation?"
"A good way to break the is to ask about the art."
"Let's for a while before the curator's talk."

Stage 2: Work the Room in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of working a networking room in the correct order.

?You ask for the other person's take on a piece and share your own candid impression.
?You arrive and take a moment to read the room before you join a group.
?You exchange details, suggest a follow-up, and move on to mingle with someone new.
?You strike up a conversation, usually with a little small talk to break the ice.
?You find common ground and slowly move from the art toward business.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

From my perspective, it's his strongest work yet.
Na minha visão, é o trabalho mais forte dele até agora.
What's your take on the large installation?
Qual é a sua opinião sobre a grande instalação?
I see your point, but I'm inclined to think it's overpriced.
Entendo o seu ponto, mas tendo a achar que está caro demais.
Let's mingle for a while before we talk business.
Vamos circular um pouco antes de falar de negócios.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want to give your own opinion of the exhibit. Which is best?
A "From my perspective, the exhibit is brilliant."
B "According to me, the exhibit is brilliant."
C "I am agree the exhibit is brilliant."
You want to invite a guest to share their opinion. Which is best?
A "What you think about the installation?"
B "What's your take on the installation?"
C "Why you not like the installation?"
You want to disagree politely with a confident statement. Which is best?
A "I see your point, but I'm inclined to think the early works are stronger."
B "No. You are wrong about the early works."
C "According to me you are wrong."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are at a gallery opening with a partner. Give your candid impression of an exhibition, using at least three different opinion phrases (strong, neutral, and hedged), and end by inviting your partner's take ("What's your take on...?").

Survival Card -- Lesson 9

1From my perspective, it's his strongest work yet.Na minha visão, é o trabalho mais forte dele até agora.
2What's your take on the large installation?Qual é a sua opinião sobre a grande instalação?
3Can I be candid? I'd argue the early pieces are more compelling.Posso ser franca? Eu diria que as obras iniciais são mais cativantes.
4I see your point, but I'm inclined to think it's overpriced.Entendo o seu ponto, mas tendo a achar que está caro demais.
5Shall we mingle for a while before we talk business?Vamos circular um pouco antes de falar de negócios?
Aula 10 -- Pre-class

Review & Integration -- Block 1 Checkpoint

Consolidate everything from aulas 1-9 and add the language that ties it together: summary phrases (to recap, to sum up, in a nutshell, on the whole, all in all, the bottom line) and linking words (firstly, moreover, however, in conclusion). Review focus: the passive voice ("the report was written", never "was wrote").
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each phrase and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

To recap -- to quickly go over the main points again
"Let me recap what we agreed in the meeting." (Recapitular/retomar)
To sum up -- to give the main idea in a few words
"To sum up, the quarter was a success." (Resumir)
In a nutshell -- very briefly, in a few words
"In a nutshell, the deal is a good one." (Em poucas palavras)
On the whole -- considering everything together; generally
"On the whole, the partners are happy." (No geral)
All in all -- after considering everything; overall
"All in all, it was a strong first block." (No fim das contas)
Key takeaway -- the single most important point to remember
"The key takeaway is to keep it simple." (Lição principal)
To consolidate -- to combine separate parts into a single strong whole
"Let's consolidate everything we learned." (Consolidar)
Moving forward -- from now on; in the future
"Moving forward, we will meet monthly." (Daqui pra frente)
In conclusion -- used to introduce your final point or summary
"In conclusion, the strategy is working." (Em conclusão)
Moreover -- in addition; what is more
"The plan is cheap. Moreover, it is fast." (Além disso)
All things considered -- after thinking about every factor
"All things considered, we should sign." (Considerando tudo)
The bottom line -- the single most important fact or result
"The bottom line is we made a profit." (O essencial/resultado final)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each phrase with its translation.

To recap
To sum up
In a nutshell
On the whole
All in all
Key takeaway
To consolidate
Moving forward
In conclusion
Moreover
All things considered
The bottom line

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Over dinner, David asks me to recap the quarter. "Firstly, the meetings ran cleanly," I say. "Moreover, the numbers were presented well, and the deal was signed on time." David nods. "However, costs rose in March," I add, "so margins were tighter." Then I close the summary: "In conclusion, on the whole it was a strong start. The key takeaway for me is confidence." David smiles. "Moving forward, Block 2 is about presentations. All things considered, you're ready." "The bottom line," I reply, "is that I want to polish one thing -- the passive. I still say 'was wrote' when I mean 'was written'."

1. Which linking word does Maria use to ADD a second positive point?
A "Moreover" -- it adds another point.
B "However" -- it adds another point.
C "Firstly" -- it adds another point.
2. Which sentence is in the passive voice?
A "The meetings ran cleanly."
B "The deal was signed on time."
C "Costs rose in March."
3. What is the correct passive form of "I still say 'was wrote'"?
A "was written" -- be + past participle.
B "was wrote" is already correct.
C "was writing" -- be + -ing.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Linking & Summarizing

GRAMMAR

No nível B2, uma boa síntese não lista fatos soltos -- ela os CONECTA com linking words e os fecha com uma frase de resumo. E lembre: a voz passiva é be + partícipio passado (written, sent, signed). / At B2, a good summary does not list loose facts -- it CONNECTS them with linking words and closes with a summary phrase. And remember: the passive is be + past participle (written, sent, signed).

FunctionLinking wordsExample
Sequence / orderFirstly... / Then... / Finally..."Firstly, we set the agenda."
Add a pointMoreover... / In addition..."Moreover, costs went down."
ContrastHowever... / On the other hand..."However, the offer was low."
Conclude / summarizeIn conclusion... / To sum up... / All in all..."In conclusion, we succeeded."

Key point / Ponto-chave: escolha a linking word pela FUNÇÃO (ordem, adição, contraste, conclusão) e feche com uma frase de resumo. Na voz passiva, use sempre o partícipio passado: "the report was written" (nunca "was wrote"), "the email was sent" (nunca "was sended"). / Choose the linking word by its FUNCTION and close with a summary phrase. In the passive, always use the past participle: "was written", never "was wrote".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word or phrase. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"To , the deal was approved by the board."
"The plan is cheap. , it is fast to roll out."
"Sales rose. , costs rose too."
"On the , it was a strong quarter."
"The report was by the finance team."
"The proposal was to the board on Monday."

Stage 2: Summarize in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of a strong business-dinner summary in the correct order.

?You give your candid opinion of a deal, hedging where you are not certain.
?You open the evening with light small talk and a warm welcome, not with business.
?You sum up the block, name your key takeaway, and look ahead to what comes next.
?You recap the quarter, summarizing the wins with clear linking words.
?You connect the separate themes, showing how the meeting, the numbers, and the negotiation fit together.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

To recap, the deal was approved by the board.
Recapitulando, o acordo foi aprovado pelo conselho.
On the whole, it was a strong quarter.
No geral, foi um trimestre forte.
The report was written by the finance team.
O relatório foi escrito pela equipe financeira.
In conclusion, the bottom line is we grew.
Em conclusão, o essencial é que crescemos.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want to open a short summary of the quarter. Which is best?
A "To recap, revenue is up and the deal was approved."
B "For recap, revenue is up and the deal was approve."
C "To recap, revenue is up and the deal was approving."
You want to report a result in the passive. Which is correct?
A "The proposal was sended to the board."
B "The proposal was sent to the board."
C "The proposal was send to the board."
You want to close your summary with an overall verdict. Which is best?
A "All in all, it was a strong first block."
B "All in all, it is a strong first block was."
C "In the all, it was a strong first block."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Sum up your own work month as if you were briefing a partner over dinner. Use at least one sequencing word, one adding word, one contrasting word, and a final summary phrase -- and report two results in the passive ("the report was written", "the deal was signed").

Survival Card -- Lesson 10

1To recap, the deal was approved by the board.Recapitulando, o acordo foi aprovado pelo conselho.
2On the whole, it was a strong quarter.No geral, foi um trimestre forte.
3Moreover, costs went down and margins improved.Além disso, os custos caíram e as margens melhoraram.
4In conclusion, the bottom line is we grew.Em conclusão, o essencial é que crescemos.
5Moving forward, the report will be written by Friday.Daqui pra frente, o relatório será escrito até sexta.
Aula 11 -- Pre-class

Conference Calls -- Managing Tech & Time Zones

Run a video call with your US partners like a pro: handle the tech (audio cutting out, frozen screens, mute, lag) and the time zones, and make polite requests with "Could you...?" and "Would you mind + -ing". Plus the passive voice for call problems (the line was dropped, you are being muted). New vocabulary: dial in, on mute, speak up, cut out, choppy, freeze, lag, bandwidth, drop, share your screen, time zone, reschedule.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Dial in -- to connect to a conference call by phone or computer
"Let me dial in to the call with Houston." (Entrar na chamada)
On mute -- with your microphone turned off, so no one can hear you
"Sorry, I was on mute. Let me start again." (No mudo)
Speak up -- to talk more loudly so others can hear you clearly
"Could you speak up? You are very quiet." (Falar mais alto)
Cut out -- when audio or video stops for a short moment, then returns
"You are cutting out -- I missed the last figure." (Cair o áudio)
Choppy -- broken and uneven, with short gaps -- said of audio or video
"The connection is a bit choppy on my end." (Picotado/falho)
Freeze -- when a video image stops moving and gets stuck
"Your screen froze for a second -- you are back now." (Congelar)
Lag -- a delay between speaking and being heard or seen
"There is a lag, so let us pause between sentences." (Atraso/lag)
Bandwidth -- the amount of data your internet can handle at once
"My bandwidth is low, so I will turn my camera off." (Largura de banda)
Drop -- when a call suddenly ends because the connection is lost
"The call dropped, but I have dialed back in." (Cair a chamada)
Share your screen -- to let everyone on the call see what is on your screen
"Would you mind sharing your screen with the slides?" (Compartilhar a tela)
Time zone -- a region of the world that shares the same local clock time
"With three time zones, we meet at my late afternoon." (Fuso horário)
Reschedule -- to change a meeting to a different date or time
"Let us reschedule the last item for tomorrow." (Reagendar)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Dial in
On mute
Speak up
Cut out
Choppy
Freeze
Lag
Bandwidth
Drop
Share your screen
Time zone
Reschedule

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

I dial in five minutes early. David is in Houston, the team is in Pittsburgh, and I am in São Paulo -- three time zones on one call. David starts, but the audio is choppy. "Could you move closer to your router?" I ask. Then it is my turn. "Would you mind sharing your screen?" he says. Suddenly my video freezes: the line was dropped for a moment. I dial in again. "Sorry, I was on mute," I say. The Pittsburgh team is being muted by the host, so they type their questions. With only thirty minutes, we agree to reschedule the rest.

1. How does Maria politely ask David to fix his choppy audio?
A With "Could you move closer to your router?" -- a polite request.
B With "You must move now."
C She says nothing about it.
2. Which form follows "Would you mind"?
A The infinitive: "Would you mind to share..."
B The -ing form: "Would you mind sharing..."
C The past tense: "Would you mind shared..."
3. How does Maria describe the connection problem, using the passive?
A "The line was dropped for a moment." -- be + past participle.
B "The line is dropping me now."
C "I dropped the line on purpose."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Polite Requests & the Passive

GRAMMAR

Em chamadas, peça as coisas com educação: "Could you" + verbo na forma base, ou "Would you mind" + verbo com -ING (gerundio). Use a voz passiva (be + particpio passado) para problemas tecnicos quando quem causou nao importa. / On calls, make polite requests: "Could you" + base verb, or "Would you mind" + the -ing form. Use the passive (be + past participle) for tech problems when the doer does not matter.

PatternFormExample
Could you...?Could you + base verb"Could you speak up?"
Would you mind...?Would you mind + verb-ing"Would you mind repeating that?"
Passive voicebe + past participle"The line was dropped."

Key point / Ponto-chave: depois de "Could you" use o verbo na forma base (speak, share); depois de "Would you mind" use o gerundio com -ING (speaking, sharing), NUNCA "to + verbo". Para problemas de chamada, a passiva (was dropped, is being muted, has been rescheduled) e natural e elegante. E diga "on mute", nunca "in mute". / After "Could you" use the base verb; after "Would you mind" use the -ing form, never "to + verb". The passive is perfect for call problems.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word or phrase. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Could you up? You are very quiet."
"Would you mind your screen?"
"The call was , but I have dialed back in."
"Sorry, I was on . Let me start again."
"You are cutting . Would you mind repeating that?"
"With three time zones, let us the rest."

Stage 2: Handle a Glitch in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of handling a tech problem on a call in the correct order.

?You suggest a fix: ask them to come off mute, to share their screen, or to turn the camera off.
?You notice the problem: the audio is choppy, or the other person has frozen.
?If nothing works, you agree to reschedule the rest for a time that suits all three time zones.
?You flag it politely: "Could you say that again?" or "Would you mind repeating the last point?"
?If the call drops, you stay calm and dial back in without panicking.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

Could you speak up a little, please?
Você poderia falar um pouco mais alto, por favor?
Would you mind sharing your screen?
Você se importaria de compartilhar a sua tela?
You are cutting out. Would you mind repeating that?
Você está falhando. Você se importaria de repetir?
The call was dropped, but I have dialed back in.
A chamada caiu, mas eu já reconectei.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want a colleague to repeat something, very politely. Which is best?
A "Would you mind repeating the last point?"
B "Would you mind to repeat the last point?"
C "You repeat the last point."
The connection just failed. How do you describe it naturally?
A "I dropped the call to everybody."
B "The call was dropped, but I am back now."
C "The call is dropping me always."
No one can hear your colleague. What do you say?
A "We cannot hear you -- you are on mute."
B "We cannot hear you -- you are in mute."
C "We cannot hear you -- you are muting."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are running a video call with your partners across three time zones, and the tech keeps failing. Describe what happens, making at least two polite requests (one with "Could you...?" and one with "Would you mind + -ing") and using the passive voice once (for example, "the line was dropped").

Survival Card -- Lesson 11

1Could you speak up a little, please?Você poderia falar um pouco mais alto, por favor?
2Would you mind sharing your screen?Você se importaria de compartilhar a sua tela?
3Sorry, I was on mute. Let me start again.Desculpe, eu estava no mudo. Deixe-me começar de novo.
4You are cutting out. Would you mind repeating that?Você está falhando. Você se importaria de repetir?
5The call was dropped, but I have dialed back in.A chamada caiu, mas eu já reconectei.
Aula 12 -- Pre-class

Fiscal and Tax Talk -- Communicating With Accountants

Talk to your accountants in English about the fiscal year: tax returns, deadlines, audits, and what is deductible. Say how long something has been true with the present perfect + for / since ("We have used this system for three years", "I have worked with them since 2015"), and use the passive voice for fiscal facts (the return has been filed, taxes are withheld at source). New vocabulary: tax return, file, withhold, deductible, audit, ledger, invoice, accrual, fiscal year, liability, reconcile, comply.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Tax return -- an official report of income and tax sent to the authorities
"We have already filed this year's tax return." (Declaração de imposto)
File -- to submit a document officially to the tax authority
"We file the return before the deadline." (Declarar / protocolar)
Withhold -- to deduct tax from a payment before the money is paid out
"Taxes are withheld at source on every dividend." (Reter na fonte)
Deductible -- an expense that can be subtracted from your taxable income
"Travel costs are deductible if you keep the receipts." (Dedutível)
Audit -- an official examination of a company's accounts and records
"We have been preparing for the audit since March." (Auditoria)
Ledger -- the book or system that records all financial transactions
"Every payment is entered in the ledger." (Livro-razão)
Invoice -- a document that requests payment for goods or services
"The supplier sent the invoice last week." (Nota fiscal / fatura)
Accrual -- revenue or cost recorded when it occurs, not when cash moves
"Under the accrual method, we record the expense now." (Regime de competência)
Fiscal year -- the 12-month period a company uses for accounting and tax
"Our fiscal year ends in December." (Ano fiscal)
Liability -- a debt or financial obligation that the company owes
"Deferred tax is recorded as a liability." (Passivo / obrigação)
Reconcile -- to make two sets of figures agree, so the totals match
"We reconcile the bank statement every month." (Conciliar)
Comply -- to act in line with the rules, laws, and regulations
"We comply with the new tax-reporting rules." (Cumprir as regras)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Tax return
File
Withhold
Deductible
Audit
Ledger
Invoice
Accrual
Fiscal year
Liability
Reconcile
Comply

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

It is the end of the fiscal year, and I am on a call with Marcelo, my accountant. I have worked with his firm since 2015, and we have used the same accounting system for three years. The good news: the tax return has been filed, two days before the deadline. The audit starts on Monday, and Marcelo has gathered the documents since the start of the month. Two things remain: the consulting fees are only deductible if we keep every invoice, and the December statement has not been reconciled with the ledger yet. Taxes are withheld at source on the dividends, so that is not a liability we still owe. We have complied with the new rules for three years, so the audit should be smooth.

1. How long has the writer worked with Marcelo's firm?
A Since 2015 -- present perfect with "since" + a starting point.
B For 2015 -- present simple.
C Since three years.
2. Which word goes with a period of time, like "three years"?
A "since" -- since three years.
B "for" -- for three years.
C Neither -- you use no word.
3. How is the completed tax return described?
A "It has been filed." -- passive: have been + past participle.
B "It is filing now."
C "It files itself."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Perfect (for / since) & the Passive

GRAMMAR

Para dizer há quanto tempo algo é verdade, use o present perfect (have/has + particpio) com FOR + um período (for three years) ou SINCE + um ponto de partida (since 2015). Use a voz passiva (be / have been + particpio) para fatos fiscais quando quem fez a ação não importa. / To say how long something has been true, use the present perfect (have/has + past participle) with FOR + a period or SINCE + a starting point. Use the passive (be / have been + past participle) for fiscal facts when the doer does not matter.

ElementFormExample
Present perfecthave / has + past participle"have worked", "has filed"
forfor + a period"for three years"
sincesince + a starting point"since 2015"
Passive voicebe / have been + past participle"The return has been filed."

Key point / Ponto-chave: para "há quanto tempo", use o present perfect (have worked), NUNCA o presente simples (work). FOR + período (for ten years); SINCE + ponto de partida (since 2015). A passiva (has been filed, are withheld) é natural no mundo fiscal. / For "how long", use the present perfect, never the present simple. FOR + a period; SINCE + a starting point. The passive is natural in fiscal English.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"We have worked with this firm 2015."
"We have used this system three years."
"The tax return has been before the deadline."
"Travel costs are if you keep the receipts."
"Every month we the bank statement."
"Taxes are at source on the dividends."

Stage 2: Order the Fiscal Call

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of a call with your accountant in the correct order.

?He reminds you of the rules: which costs are deductible, and how taxes are withheld at source.
?The accountant tells you what has been done: the return has been filed and the books have been closed.
?You agree on a date to review everything together, well before the fiscal year closes.
?He flags what is still open, such as a bank statement that has not been reconciled yet.
?He gives you the deadline and asks you to send any missing invoices before the audit.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

We have worked with this firm since 2015.
Nós trabalhamos com esta firma desde 2015.
The tax return has been filed before the deadline.
A declaração foi entregue antes do prazo.
Are these costs deductible this fiscal year?
Estes custos são dedutíveis neste ano fiscal?
The statement has not been reconciled yet.
O extrato ainda não foi conciliado.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want to say how long you have used the same system (three years). Which is best?
A "We have used the same system for three years."
B "We have used the same system since three years."
C "We use the same system for three years."
The return is done. How do you report it naturally to your accountant?
A "I am filing the return since yesterday."
B "The return has been filed already."
C "The return files before the deadline."
You want to know when the audits began. Which question is correct?
A "How long have the accounts been audited?"
B "How long the accounts are audited?"
C "Since how long the accounts audit?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are on a call with your accountant at the end of the fiscal year.
